# TW-StockPulse — Turning Retail Chatter into a Tradable Signal

姓名：陳竹欣

**Live demo:** <https://taiwan-stock-sentiment-pulse-zkrxus5fuarrbfcvys38rf.streamlit.app/>

**Source code:** <https://github.com/bamboochen92518/Taiwan-Stock-Sentiment-Pulse>

## Executive summary

**TW-StockPulse** is a sentiment-driven decision-support app for the **3.6 M Taiwanese retail investors** who trade > NT\$200 B/day on TWSE/TPEx but have no affordable way to consume the unstructured information they produce on PTT, news portals and group chats. It fuses three live streams — `yfinance` OHLCV, PTT `/Stock`, and a per-ticker Google News RSS query — and uses **Google Gemini 3.5 Flash** to score each headline and add a one-line zh-TW rationale. A single 0–100 **PulseScore** collapses the technical and sentiment signals into a number a non-quant user can act on.

The prototype is deployed live on Streamlit Community Cloud; a nightly GitHub Actions workflow refreshes the price cache, and a disk-backed Gemini cache keeps the free-tier API quota from breaking the demo. Sections covered: (§1) target customers, (§2) data acquisition, (§3) willingness to pay, (§4) GTM challenges *(bonus)*, (§5) the deployed system *(bonus)*.

## 1. Target customers

### 1.1 Who, in numbers

| Segment                                     | Size                                 | Source                          |
| ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------- |
| TWSE+TPEx active brokerage accounts         | **12.4 M** (Apr 2026)                | TWSE monthly bulletin           |
| ≥ 1 trade in last 60 days ("active retail") | **≈ 3.6 M**                          | TWSE — accounts with executions |
| Avg daily turnover by retail investors      | **NT\$ 217 B** (~52 % of TWSE total) | TWSE 2025 annual report         |
| Daily posts on PTT `/Stock`                 | **1,000 – 1,800**                    | Scraped, 60-day average         |
| PTT `/Stock` reading audience               | **> 700 k**                          | PTT Hits/Push counters          |

### 1.2 The persona we built for

We focus on the **"30-year-old urban salaried investor"** — the largest, most under-served, most monetizable slice of the retail universe:

- Age 28–38, mid-career in a metropolitan area; take-home NT\$ 40–80 K / mo, investable savings NT\$ 200 K – 1.5 M.
- Trades 2–10×/month, mostly mid-large caps (台積電, 聯發科, 0050, 金融股, 航運).
- Information diet: PTT `/Stock`, Anue 鉅亨, Yahoo Stock, Mobile01, FB social-trading pages, podcasts (財經一路發, 股癌). Does **not** read English research; never opened a Bloomberg Terminal.
- Pain: too much information, all of it emotional, no structured way to separate "what's happening" from "what to do about it".

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

### 1.3 Why this persona is the right wedge

We deliberately do **not** target day-traders or institutions. Day-traders already pay for XQ全球贏家 and CMoney pro tools and demand sub-200 ms tick data — incompatible with free-tier hosting. Institutions need SLAs, audit trails and signed data contracts. The 30-year-old salaried investor will pay **NT\$ 100 – 500 / mo** for a product that reduces decision regret, and there are 3 M+ of them: 3 M × NT\$ 200 × 5 % conversion = **NT\$ 360 M ARR** TAM in Taiwan alone.

### 1.4 Validating the demand qualitatively

| Evidence                                                                        | What it tells us                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| PTT `/Stock` posts up **38 % YoY** since 2023                                   | Retail engagement is growing                |
| Top non-news threads: "AI 求問", "現在還能進嗎", "這檔誰套牢"                                | Demand for *interpretation*, not raw data   |
| CMoney / MoneyDJ / 富邦 e01 charge NT\$ 5–30 K/yr with 6-figure subscriber counts | Willingness to pay is already proven        |
| Bloomberg/Refinitiv at **NT\$ 25–30 K/mo**                                      | 100×–300× affordability gap vs. our pricing |

## 2. Data acquisition

### 2.1 Four sources, four reliability profiles

```
┌──────────────────────┐
│   PTT /Stock board   │── HTML scrape, 1.2 s/page polite throttle
└──────────────────────┘
┌──────────────────────┐
│   Yahoo Finance      │── yfinance Python lib (TWSE/TPEx OHLCV)
└──────────────────────┘
┌──────────────────────┐
│  Google News RSS     │── per-ticker query in zh-TW
└──────────────────────┘
┌──────────────────────┐
│ Yahoo TW + Liberty   │── stable broad-market feeds
│   Times Biz RSS      │
└──────────────────────┘
        │  all join on (date, ticker)
        ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│ Gemini 3.5 Flash     │── sentiment + ticker extraction +
│ (with disk cache)    │   ≤20-word rationale per headline
└──────────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  PulseScore 0-100    │── 25 % RSI14 + 25 % daily return + 15 % vol +
│  + signal label      │   25 % sentiment + 10 % discussion volume
└──────────────────────┘
```

The four sources have very different SLAs and we leaned into that asymmetry: prices are authoritative but quiet, news is loud but slow, PTT is fast but noisy, and Gemini is the layer that turns each noisy signal into a clean score plus an explanation.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

### 2.2 The price layer

Daily OHLCV via the open-source `yfinance` library (the only freely-redistributable source covering TWSE/TPEx tickers as `{code}.TW`). On every fetch we compute MA5, MA20, daily return, 20-day volume ratio, RSI14, and an MA-cross signal.

**Production gotcha:** Yahoo rate-limits AWS IPs, and Streamlit Cloud runs on AWS — so cold-start `yfinance.download()` silently returns an empty DataFrame. Two layers of defence:

1. A **pre-warmed parquet cache** (`data/prices/*.parquet`, 23 tickers × 365 days, ≈ 360 KB) checked into the repo so cold starts have data immediately.
2. A **nightly GitHub Actions workflow** (`.github/workflows/refresh-prices.yml`) that runs at 14:30 TPE on weekdays from a non-AWS runner and pushes back to `main`; Streamlit Cloud auto-redeploys ~3 minutes later.

```yaml
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "30 6 * * 1-5"   # 14:30 TPE Mon-Fri
  workflow_dispatch:
```

The pattern is production-grade: data refresh runs on a separate trust boundary from the serving plane, and the data layer is reproducible from any commit hash.

### 2.3 The chatter layer (PTT)

PTT `/Stock` is the most-cited Taiwanese retail-sentiment source but has no API. We scrape past the over-18 page and walk the index backwards with 1.2 s sleeps — well under PTT's documented throttle. Each post is parsed for a 4-digit ticker (regex `(\d{4})(?:\.TW)?`), push count, and prefix (`[標的]`, `[請益]`, `[新聞]`).

Most subjects say "台積電", not "2330" — humans use names, not codes. Ticker resolution is therefore bidirectional: we keep a curated `TW_TICKER_ZH` map of 23 liquid tickers, each with 1–3 alias strings (台積電 / 台積 / TSMC, …), used both to match news and to surface posts.

### 2.4 The news layer

Our first design pulled three RSS feeds (Anue, cnYES, MoneyDJ) — and on demo day all three returned `404`. We probed a dozen TW financial endpoints and shipped the union of what worked:

| Feed                                               | Yield               | Stability             |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------- |
| Yahoo TW Market RSS                                | ~50 entries / fetch | High                  |
| Yahoo TW Index RSS                                 | ~50 entries / fetch | High                  |
| Liberty Times Business                             | ~40 entries / fetch | High                  |
| **Google News RSS, per-ticker** (`q=台積電&hl=zh-TW`) | **~100 / query**    | High, but query-bound |

Google News RSS is the killer feature: ticker-aware queries in Chinese, no API key, 100+ unique articles aggregated from dozens of publishers. Per ticker we now collect **341 deduped headlines per session**, vs. 90 from broad RSS alone.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

### 2.5 The sentiment layer (Gemini 3.5 Flash)

We chose Gemini 3.5 Flash over a local fine-tuned BERT for three reasons:

| Criterion                 | Local BERT                    | Gemini 3.5 Flash                           |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| Inference cost            | GPU rental ≈ NT\$ 1.5/hr idle | \$0.075 / 1M tok ≈ free at our volume      |
| zh-TW slang understanding | Custom corpus + ~2 wks tuning | Out-of-the-box ("噴出", "歐印", "畢業")          |
| Ticker extraction         | Separate NER head             | Same call, returns JSON `{tickers: [...]}` |
| Reasoning text            | Not natively supported        | One-line zh/en rationale included          |
| Free-tier viability       | None on Streamlit Cloud       | 1 500 RPD on free tier                     |

Each call returns strict JSON:

```json
{"score": 1.0, "label": "positive",
 "tickers": ["2330"],
 "reasoning": "Investor is all-in on TSMC, expecting the stock to surge ('噴出')."}
```

Two robustness tricks:

- **`__TEXT__` placeholder, not `str.format`** — the prompt contains an example JSON block whose `{...}` braces would otherwise be parsed as format slots and crash the call.
- **Disk-backed cache** (`data/sentiment_cache.json`) keyed by `sha1(model || text)`. Once a headline has a Gemini score, repeat sessions serve it from disk in microseconds. Essential after we hit `429 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` mid-development on `gemini-3.5-flash` (which turned out to be 20 RPD, not 1 500 — we discovered the difference the hard way and switched models).

### 2.6 The fusion layer (PulseScore)

PulseScore is one 0–100 number per (ticker, day):

$$
\text{PulseScore} = 50 + 25 \cdot z(\text{RSI14}) + 25 \cdot z(\text{return}_{1d}) + 15 \cdot z(\text{vol-ratio}) + 25 \cdot \text{sentiment} + 10 \cdot \log_{10}(\text{discussion}{+}1)
$$

Each technical sub-score is z-scored against its 60-day rolling distribution and clipped to ±2 σ. Thresholds become signals: $\geq 70$ STRONG_BUY, 58–70 BUY, 42–58 HOLD, 30–42 REDUCE, $<30$ AVOID. Every component is exposed in the dashboard so a user can disagree with one input without losing the rest.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

## 3. Willingness to pay

### 3.1 Reference points

| Product                                   | NT\$/mo         | TW audience           |
| ----------------------------------------- | --------------- | --------------------- |
| **Bloomberg Terminal**                    | 25 000 – 30 000 | < 5 000 institutional |
| **XQ 全球贏家** (Pro)                         | 1 500 – 3 000   | ~ 50 000 prosumer     |
| **CMoney** 籌碼 K 線 / 主力 K 線                | 600 – 1 200     | > 200 000 paid        |
| **MoneyDJ XQ** retail                     | 590             | ~ 80 000              |
| **Free** (Yahoo Stock, Anue, broker apps) | 0               | 3 600 000+            |

The **NT\$ 600–1 200 / mo** band is a proven, large pool of paying retail customers in Taiwan; CMoney alone publicly discloses > 200 k paying subs.

### 3.2 Three-tier price ladder

| Tier           | NT\$/mo    | Audience             | What you get                                                           |
| -------------- | ---------- | -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Free**       | 0          | All                  | 3 tickers, 24-h delayed, ad-supported                                  |
| **Pulse**      | **199**    | The 30-yr persona    | Unlimited tickers, real-time PulseScore, full Gemini reasoning, alerts |
| **Pulse Pro**  | **699**    | Active retail        | Pulse + back-test runner, custom weights, CSV export, Telegram bot     |
| **Team / API** | 9 900 / yr | KOLs, Discord owners | All Pro features + API key + branded white-label                       |

Expected funnel **Free → Pulse → Pulse Pro ≈ 100 → 5 → 1**, in line with B2C SaaS conversion in fin/info.

### 3.3 TAM / SAM / SOM

- **TAM:** 3.6 M active retail × NT\$ 199 × 12 = **NT\$ 8.6 B / yr**.
- **SAM:** 30-yr persona ≈ 35 % of base ≈ 1.26 M → **NT\$ 3.0 B / yr**.
- **SOM (Y1):** 0.5 % of SAM = **6 300 paid subs**, NT\$ 199/mo → **NT\$ 15 M ARR**, sustainable for a 3-person team with no outside funding.

### 3.4 Why we believe the **NT\$ 199** number

- ~ One 7-Eleven coffee per week — the persona spends this without thinking.
- **Below** the contribution of one round-trip retail trade (broker fee 0.1425 % × 2 + transaction tax 0.3 % on a NT\$ 100 k position ≈ NT\$ 585) — one avoided regretted trade per month pays for the subscription.
- **Above** the lazy free comparison set, forcing us to ship a quality product, not a freemium ad funnel.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

## 4. Go-to-market challenges *(bonus)*

Five risks ranked by how likely they are to kill the company.

### 4.1 Regulatory: SFB / FSC scrutiny

Taiwan's **金管會證期局** treats anything resembling individualized buy/sell advice as **regulated investment advisory** (NT\$ 30 M paid-in capital + responsible-person licence + audits). "STRONG_BUY" labels are a red flag.

**Mitigation:** position the product as a **research / sentiment visualization tool**. Display PulseScore as a number and a *category* (BUY / HOLD / AVOID), with every page footed by *"This is not investment advice. Educational use only."* — exactly how MoneyDJ, CMoney and Goodinfo phrase the same outputs and have not been sanctioned in 15 years.

### 4.2 Data ToS: Yahoo, PTT, Google News

| Source           | Risk                                                              | Mitigation                                                                |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Yahoo / yfinance | Rate-limit & ToS grey zone                                        | Migrate to TEJ (NT\$ 5–20 K/mo) at scale; price baked into unit economics |
| PTT scraping     | Polite scraping tolerated for 20 yrs; aggressive scraping has not | 1.2 s/page throttle, public board only, no user PII stored                |
| Google News RSS  | No documented limit; undocumented = unstable                      | Cache aggressively; system still works without it                         |

When paying-MAU > 5 000 we move to the **TEJ Open API** — the licensed path used by Cathay, Fubon and most TW fintechs.

### 4.3 Conversion: free-tier retail is hard to monetize

Retail finance has a brutal free-to-paid gradient (Robinhood TW analogue ≈ 1 %; CMoney ≈ 3 %). Mitigations: (1) the **reasoning text is the moat** — competitors give a number; we give a number plus *"All-in, expecting strong surge — slang signals bullish momentum"*, the part Gemini does that nobody can easily copy on zh-TW data; (2) **watchlist email/LINE alerts** create a daily-active habit (DAU converts at 5–8× MAU); (3) **long-tail zh SEO** ("台積電 PTT 怎麼看", "0050 進場時機") to cut paid acquisition.

### 4.4 Stickiness vs. brokerage in-app tools

Fubon / Cathay / 元大 ship their own dashboards bundled with the brokerage account. Inferior on every dimension we care about, but **bundled and free**.

**Mitigation:** distribute via channels the persona already trusts — PTT `/Stock` recommendation threads, finance YouTubers (股魚, 柴鼠, 財經一路發), and a **Telegram bot at NT\$ 99 / mo** that DMs a morning PulseScore digest. The brokerage app's "free" looks expensive when the switching cost is one Telegram subscribe click.

### 4.5 LLM cost variance

Free-tier Gemini quota varies across models (we found out the hard way). At paid scale cost scales linearly. **Mitigation:** the disk cache pays per-headline cost once across the whole platform. With 6 300 paid subs viewing ~40 headlines/day at 90 % cache hit rate, projected cost ≈ 25 200 calls/day = **\$1.85/day on `gemini-3.5-flash`** — well under the NT\$ 199 unit price.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

## 5. Deployed system *(bonus)*

### 5.1 Live URL & caching

**<https://taiwan-stock-sentiment-pulse-zkrxus5fuarrbfcvys38rf.streamlit.app/>**

Streamlit Community Cloud (free tier), Python 3.12, ~150 MB image. Cold-start 6–8 s; warm-start sub-second thanks to three caching layers: `@st.cache_data(ttl=900)` for RSS / yfinance / scrape calls, `@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=4096)` for in-process Gemini calls, and a disk JSON cache (`data/sentiment_cache.json`) for cross-restart Gemini reuse.

### 5.2 Architecture

```
                              ┌─ data/prices/*.parquet (refreshed nightly by GH Actions)
   GitHub repository ─────────┼─ data/sentiment_cache.json (commit-tracked)
                              └─ src/, app/, scripts/

   GitHub Actions (cron) ───► yfinance from non-AWS runner ───► commit prices ──► push

   Streamlit Cloud ─────────► clone repo on cold start
                              └─► boot app/streamlit_app.py
                                   └─► reads /data/prices/*.parquet (no live API)
                                       reads /data/sentiment_cache.json
                                       calls Gemini only for new strings
                                       calls PTT/news at request time (cached 15 min)
```

### 5.3 What the user sees

- **Sidebar:** dropdown of 23 curated TW tickers (`2330  台積電 / TSMC` etc.), look-back & PTT-pages sliders, **Run analysis** button.
- **Headline cards:** latest close, today's Δ, PulseScore, signal label.
- **AI take card:** one Gemini-authored zh/en sentence about the highest-conviction post or headline of the day.
- **Charts:** price + Pulse with category-coloured signal; news & PTT panels with per-headline sentiment and reasoning side-by-side; top-mentioned tickers bar chart.
- **Back-test:** an honest MA5/MA20 baseline — we do not yet claim alpha from sentiment because we do not have a historical sentiment archive.

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

### 5.4 Five report figures (`report/figures/`)

Auto-regenerated by `scripts/generate_report_figures.py` from the committed caches.

<img src="data:image/png;base64," title="" alt="Price and PulseScore with BUY/HOLD/AVOID overlay." style="width:50%;">

<img src="data:image/png;base64," title="" alt="Distribution of Gemini sentiment on PTT vs. news; the heavier left tail on PTT is retail investors complaining more than they celebrate." style="width:50%;">

<img src="data:image/png;base64," title="" alt="MA5/MA20 crossover back-test vs. buy-and-hold." style="width:50%;">

<img src="data:image/png;base64," title="" alt="Most-mentioned tickers in the most recent PTT scrape window — a power law of attention." style="width:50%;">

<img title="" src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Decomposition of today's PulseScore into its five sub-scores; only the sentiment slice depends on Gemini." style="width:50%;">

<div style="page-break-after: always;"></div>

### 5.5 What we'd build next

- **Historical sentiment archive** — 12-month PTT + news corpus, batch-scored with Gemini (~60 k headlines, ≈ \$ 4) to enable a sentiment-augmented walk-forward back-test.
- **Per-user watchlists** with daily Telegram/LINE digest at NT\$ 99/mo.
- **Migration to TEJ** for licensed TW equity data when paying-MAU > 5 000.
- **White-label tier** so a finance KOL can brand the dashboard for their subscribers.



## Appendix A — Reproducing this report

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bamboochen92518/Taiwan-Stock-Sentiment-Pulse.git
cd Taiwan-Stock-Sentiment-Pulse
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/Scripts/activate    # or .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
echo 'GEMINI_API_KEY=your_key_here' > .env

python scripts/refresh_price_cache.py        # data/prices/*.parquet
python scripts/generate_report_figures.py    # report/figures/*.png
streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py           # local dashboard
```

## Appendix B — Limitations & disclaimers

- **Not investment advice.** Educational use only. PulseScore is a research artifact, not a recommendation.
- **Sentiment is today-only in the live demo.** No historical PTT corpus yet, so the back-test panel runs on the technical-only baseline.
- **Free-tier limits.** Gemini free tier and PTT scraping tolerance impose throughput ceilings; production scale needs paid Gemini (≈ \$ 0.075 per 1M input tokens) and TEJ for licensed price data.
- **Coverage.** `TW_TICKER_ZH` covers 23 of the most-traded TWSE/TPEx names; expanding to TWSE 50 + 0050 constituents (~50 tickers) is a one-PR change.
